zh.lifeSummary: 當William Sidney IV於大約1417年出生在英國英格蘭素瑞斯托克 達阿波農時，他的父親William Sidney III30歲，母親Elizabeth Ceselye Mitchell23歲。他於1456年之前與Thomasine Barrington結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1462年10月22日在英國英格蘭素瑞享年46歲。

nameMeanings.name: Sidney
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: habitational name from Sidney Farm in Alfold (Surrey), from Old English sīd ‘large, spacious, long’ (dative sīdan) + ēg ‘island’.
possibly also a habitational name from any of the places called Saint-Denis in Normandy, named from the dedication of their churches to Saint Dionysius (see   Dennis  ).

variants.name: Dennis
meanings: English: from the Middle English (Old French) personal name Denis(e), Dinis(e), vernacular forms of Latin Dionysius, Greek Dionysios ‘(follower) of Dionysos’, an eastern god introduced to the classical pantheon at a relatively late date and bearing a name of probably Semitic origin. The name was borne by various early Christian saints, including Saint Denis, the martyred 3rd-century bishop of Paris who became the patron saint of France. From the 12th century onward, the popularity of the name in England (and of its cognates, mainly Denis , in the continental Europe) seems to have been largely due to French influence. The feminine form Dionysia (in vernacular English likewise Den(n)is) is also found, and some examples of the surname may originate from it.
English: from Middle English deneis, a variant of danais ‘Danish’, with the vowel influenced by Middle English denshe (Old English denisc). Compare Dence .

Irish (mainly Dublin and Cork): of the same origin as 1 and 2 above, sometimes an alternative form to Donohue but more often to McDonough , since the personal name Donnchadh was Anglicized as Donough or Denis.

variants.name: Sydney
meanings: English and Scottish (Midlothian): variant of   Sidney  .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When William Sidney IV was born about 1417, in Stoke D'abernon, Surrey, England, United Kingdom, his father, William Sidney III, was 30 and his mother, Elizabeth Ceselye Mitchell, was 23. He married Thomasine Barrington before 1456. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. He died on 22 October 1462, in Surrey, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 46.

Il a épousé Thomasine Barrington avant 1456. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 22 octobre 1462, à Surrey, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 46 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: William Sidney IV(이)가 대략 1417년 영국 잉글랜드 수리 스토크 드'압른넌에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William Sidney III(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Ceselye Mitchell(은)는 23세였다. 그는 1456년 이전 Thomasine Barrington(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1462년 10월 22일 영국 잉글랜드 수리에서 46세의 나이로 사망했다.
